title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 18.
content:
There was an Old Man of Madras,
    Who rode on a cream-coloured ass;
    But the length of its ears,
    So promoted his fears,
    That it killed that Old Man of Madras.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 18.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
